title: Irregular Verbs
note: 本词表不含情态动词。摘自九年级各单元附录。
noteEn: Modal verbs are not included. Sourced from Grade 9 appendix pages.

verbs.exampleVerb: I awake early on school days.
verbs.examplePast: She awoke to bright sunlight.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: The baby has awoken from a long nap.

verbs.exampleVerb: I am ready for the lesson.
verbs.examplePast: She was happy yesterday.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: They have been friends for years.

verbs.exampleVerb: Trees bear fruit in autumn.
verbs.examplePast: The tree bore many apples last year.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: A star has borne her name for centuries.

relatedForms.exampleSentence: The birth of the baby brought joy.

verbs.exampleVerb: Our team can beat strong rivals.
verbs.examplePast: He beat the drum loudly.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: She has beaten her personal record.

verbs.exampleVerb: Children become more independent.
verbs.examplePast: He became a doctor in 2020.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: She has become a better speaker.

verbs.exampleVerb: Lessons begin at eight o'clock.
verbs.examplePast: The film began ten minutes ago.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: We have begun a new unit today.

verbs.exampleVerb: Small cuts bleed for a short time.
verbs.examplePast: His knee bled after the fall.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: The wound has bled too much.

verbs.exampleVerb: The wind blows from the east.
verbs.examplePast: The wind blew the papers away.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: The leaves have been blown off the trees.

verbs.exampleVerb: Please do not break the glass.
verbs.examplePast: He broke his pencil during the test.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: The window has been broken.

verbs.exampleVerb: Bring your textbook tomorrow.
verbs.examplePast: She brought flowers to the party.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: He has brought good news.

verbs.exampleVerb: They build houses near the river.
verbs.examplePast: We built a model in class.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: The bridge has been built this year.

relatedForms.exampleSentence: The school building is very old.

verbs.exampleVerb: Wood burns quickly in dry weather.
verbs.examplePast: The candle burned all night.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: The paper has burned to ash.

verbs.exampleVerb: I buy lunch at the school canteen.
verbs.examplePast: She bought a new dictionary.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: We have bought enough supplies.

verbs.exampleVerb: Try to catch the ball with both hands.
verbs.examplePast: The police caught the thief.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: He has caught a bad cold.

verbs.exampleVerb: I choose the answer carefully.
verbs.examplePast: She chose the red dress.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: He has chosen his university course.

verbs.exampleVerb: They come to school by bus.
verbs.examplePast: My friend came to visit me.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: Spring has come at last.

verbs.exampleVerb: Good books cost a little money.
verbs.examplePast: The trip cost more than we expected.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: The repair has cost a lot of time.

verbs.exampleVerb: Be careful when you cut paper.
verbs.examplePast: She cut her finger slightly.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: He has cut the cake into pieces.

verbs.exampleVerb: Teachers deal with many questions.
verbs.examplePast: He dealt the cards quickly.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: We have dealt with the problem.

verbs.exampleVerb: Farmers dig the soil in spring.
verbs.examplePast: The dog dug a hole in the garden.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: They have dug a new well.

verbs.exampleVerb: I do my homework after dinner.
verbs.examplePast: She did well in the exam.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: He has done his best.

verbs.exampleVerb: She draws pictures in her notebook.
verbs.examplePast: He drew a map of the town.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: The artist has drawn many portraits.

verbs.exampleVerb: I dream about travel sometimes.
verbs.examplePast: She dreamt of becoming a writer.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: He has dreamed of this day for years.

verbs.exampleVerb: We drink water after exercise.
verbs.examplePast: He drank a cup of tea.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: They have drunk all the juice.

verbs.exampleVerb: My father drives to work.
verbs.examplePast: She drove us to the museum.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: He has driven across the country.

verbs.exampleVerb: We eat lunch at noon.
verbs.examplePast: She ate an apple after school.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: He has eaten all the soup.

verbs.exampleVerb: Leaves fall in autumn.
verbs.examplePast: He fell on the icy path.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: Snow has fallen all night.

verbs.exampleVerb: Birds feed their young in spring.
verbs.examplePast: She fed the cat this morning.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: The baby has been fed already.

verbs.exampleVerb: I feel happy today.
verbs.examplePast: She felt nervous before the test.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: We have felt better since yesterday.

verbs.exampleVerb: They fight for what is right.
verbs.examplePast: The team fought hard and won.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: Soldiers have fought for peace.

verbs.exampleVerb: I find my keys on the desk.
verbs.examplePast: She found a wallet in the park.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: He has found a new hobby.

verbs.exampleVerb: Birds fly south in winter.
verbs.examplePast: We flew to London last summer.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: She has flown alone before.

verbs.exampleVerb: Please do not forget your homework.
verbs.examplePast: I forgot his name for a moment.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: She has forgotten the password.

verbs.exampleVerb: Water freezes at zero degrees.
verbs.examplePast: The lake froze last winter.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: The ground has frozen overnight.

verbs.exampleVerb: I get up at six every day.
verbs.examplePast: She got a letter from her friend.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: He has got better at maths.

verbs.exampleVerb: Teachers give us useful advice.
verbs.examplePast: She gave me a beautiful book.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: He has given his best effort.

verbs.exampleVerb: We go to school by bus.
verbs.examplePast: They went to the cinema yesterday.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: She has gone to visit her aunt.

verbs.exampleVerb: Plants grow quickly in summer.
verbs.examplePast: He grew taller last year.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: Trees have grown along the river.

verbs.exampleVerb: Pictures hang on the classroom wall.
verbs.examplePast: She hung her coat on the hook.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: The banner has hung there for weeks.

verbs.exampleVerb: I have two brothers.
verbs.examplePast: We had a great time at the party.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: She has had this bag for years.

verbs.exampleVerb: I hear music from next door.
verbs.examplePast: We heard thunder in the distance.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: Have you heard the good news?

verbs.exampleVerb: Children hide behind the door.
verbs.examplePast: The cat hid under the bed.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: The treasure has been hidden for years.

verbs.exampleVerb: Be careful not to hit the lamp.
verbs.examplePast: The ball hit the window lightly.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: He has hit the target three times.

verbs.exampleVerb: Please hold the door open.
verbs.examplePast: The school held a sports day.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: She has held the record since 2024.

verbs.exampleVerb: Sharp words can hurt people.
verbs.examplePast: He hurt his ankle during the game.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: My leg has hurt since yesterday.

verbs.exampleVerb: Keep quiet in the library.
verbs.examplePast: She kept a diary for two years.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: He has kept his promise.

verbs.exampleVerb: I know the answer to this question.
verbs.examplePast: She knew the truth at last.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: They have known each other for years.

verbs.exampleVerb: Lay the books on the table.
verbs.examplePast: The hen laid an egg this morning.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: She has laid out the maps.

verbs.exampleVerb: Good leaders lead by example.
verbs.examplePast: He led the team to victory.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: The path has led us to the lake.

verbs.exampleVerb: We learn English at school.
verbs.examplePast: She learnt a new song yesterday.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: He has learned a lot this term.

verbs.exampleVerb: I leave home at seven thirty.
verbs.examplePast: They left the city last month.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: She has left a message for you.

verbs.exampleVerb: Can you lend me your pen?
verbs.examplePast: She lent him her dictionary.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: He has lent me money before.

verbs.exampleVerb: Let me help you with that.
verbs.examplePast: They let us use the computer room.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: She has let the dog out already.

verbs.exampleVerb: Lie down and rest for a while.
verbs.examplePast: He lay on the grass and looked up.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: She has lain awake all night.

verbs.exampleVerb: Candles light the room softly.
verbs.examplePast: She lit the lamp before reading.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: The street has been lit with lanterns.

verbs.exampleVerb: Do not lose your student card.
verbs.examplePast: We lost the match by one point.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: He has lost his wallet again.

verbs.exampleVerb: She makes model planes in her free time.
verbs.examplePast: He made a cake for his mother.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: They have made great progress.

verbs.exampleVerb: What does this word mean?
verbs.examplePast: I meant to call you earlier.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: She has meant well all along.

verbs.exampleVerb: We meet our friends after school.
verbs.examplePast: I met her at the library.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: Have you met the new teacher?

verbs.exampleVerb: Do not mistake kindness for weakness.
verbs.examplePast: I mistook him for his brother.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: She has mistaken the date.

verbs.exampleVerb: I pay for lunch with my card.
verbs.examplePast: She paid the bill yesterday.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: He has paid all the fees.

verbs.exampleVerb: Put your bag under the desk.
verbs.examplePast: She put the vase on the shelf.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: He has put the tools away.

verbs.verbFormsNote: 发音：原形 /riːd/，过去式/过去分词 /red/
verbs.exampleVerb: I read books every evening.
verbs.examplePast: She read the poem aloud in class.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: He has read three novels this month.

verbs.exampleVerb: We ride bikes to the park.
verbs.examplePast: She rode a horse on the farm.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: He has ridden the subway alone.

verbs.exampleVerb: Phones ring during break time.
verbs.examplePast: The bell rang at eight o'clock.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: The alarm has rung twice already.

verbs.exampleVerb: The sun rises in the east.
verbs.examplePast: Prices rose sharply last year.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: The river has risen after the rain.

verbs.exampleVerb: Children run in the playground.
verbs.examplePast: She ran to catch the bus.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: He has run five kilometres today.

verbs.exampleVerb: What did you say just now?
verbs.examplePast: She said hello to everyone.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: He has said sorry many times.

verbs.exampleVerb: I see a bird in the tree.
verbs.examplePast: We saw a film last weekend.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: She has seen that play before.

verbs.exampleVerb: They sell fresh fruit at the market.
verbs.examplePast: He sold his old bike online.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: The shop has sold out of tickets.

verbs.exampleVerb: Please send me the file by email.
verbs.examplePast: She sent a postcard from Paris.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: He has sent the package already.

verbs.exampleVerb: Set the alarm for six o'clock.
verbs.examplePast: The sun set behind the hills.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: She has set a new school record.

verbs.exampleVerb: Shake the bottle before you open it.
verbs.examplePast: The earthquake shook the whole city.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: He has shaken hands with the guest.

verbs.exampleVerb: Stars shine brightly at night.
verbs.examplePast: The sun shone all afternoon.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: Her eyes have shone with joy.

verbs.exampleVerb: Photographers shoot pictures of nature.
verbs.examplePast: He shot an arrow at the target.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: She has shot a short video.

verbs.exampleVerb: Maps show the way to the museum.
verbs.examplePast: She showed us her drawings.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: He has shown great courage.

verbs.exampleVerb: Shut the window, please.
verbs.examplePast: She shut the book and stood up.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: The gate has shut automatically.

verbs.exampleVerb: Birds sing in the morning.
verbs.examplePast: She sang at the school concert.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: He has sung this song many times.

verbs.exampleVerb: Please sit down and listen.
verbs.examplePast: We sat near the front row.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: She has sat here since eight.

verbs.exampleVerb: Babies sleep for many hours.
verbs.examplePast: I slept well last night.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: He has slept too little this week.

verbs.exampleVerb: Flowers smell sweet in spring.
verbs.examplePast: Something smelt burning in the kitchen.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: The room has smelt fresh since we cleaned it.

verbs.exampleVerb: She speaks English very clearly.
verbs.examplePast: He spoke to the class confidently.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: They have spoken about the plan.

verbs.exampleVerb: Cars speed along the highway.
verbs.examplePast: Time sped by during the holiday.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: The train has sped through the tunnel.

verbs.exampleVerb: How do you spell your name?
verbs.examplePast: She spelt every word correctly.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: He has spelt the word wrong twice.

verbs.exampleVerb: I spend an hour on homework daily.
verbs.examplePast: She spent the weekend with her cousins.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: We have spent all our pocket money.

verbs.exampleVerb: News spreads quickly online.
verbs.examplePast: She spread butter on the bread.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: The fire has spread to the forest.

verbs.exampleVerb: Please stand in a straight line.
verbs.examplePast: He stood by the window silently.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: She has stood first in her class.

verbs.exampleVerb: It is wrong to steal from others.
verbs.examplePast: Someone stole his bicycle.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: Thieves have stolen valuable art.

verbs.exampleVerb: Glue sticks paper together firmly.
verbs.examplePast: The key stuck in the lock.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: The label has stuck to the bottle.

verbs.exampleVerb: Workers sweep the street every morning.
verbs.examplePast: She swept the floor after class.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: He has swept the path clear of leaves.

verbs.exampleVerb: Fish swim in the clear river.
verbs.examplePast: We swam in the lake yesterday.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: She has swum across the pool twice.

verbs.exampleVerb: Take your umbrella with you.
verbs.examplePast: She took a photo of the sunset.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: He has taken the wrong bus.

verbs.exampleVerb: Teachers teach us many skills.
verbs.examplePast: She taught me how to cook.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: He has taught here for ten years.

verbs.exampleVerb: Tell me the truth, please.
verbs.examplePast: She told a funny story.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: He has told us the plan already.

verbs.exampleVerb: I think this idea is useful.
verbs.examplePast: She thought about the problem carefully.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: We have thought it over twice.

verbs.exampleVerb: Throw the ball to your partner.
verbs.examplePast: He threw the paper into the bin.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: She has thrown away old clothes.

verbs.exampleVerb: Do you understand the question?
verbs.examplePast: I understood the lesson finally.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: She has understood the rule clearly.

verbs.exampleVerb: I wake at six on weekdays.
verbs.examplePast: She woke to the sound of rain.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: He has woken up early today.

verbs.exampleVerb: Students wear uniforms at our school.
verbs.examplePast: She wore a red scarf yesterday.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: He has worn these shoes for years.

verbs.exampleVerb: Our team can win with hard work.
verbs.examplePast: She won first prize in the contest.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: He has won three games this week.

verbs.exampleVerb: I write notes in my notebook.
verbs.examplePast: She wrote a letter to her friend.
verbs.examplePastParticiple: He has written three essays this month.
